speaker: Isabel Schnabel
tone_summary: Schnabel is the most overtly hawkish Executive Board member in this window. Her March 6 US Monetary Policy Forum speech is the most policy-relevant readable text: she directly pushes back against calls for a dual mandate, arguing that in a world of frequent supply-side shocks, the paramount challenge is maintaining a credible commitment to price stability — explicitly invoking the 1960s-70s lesson of central banks that subordinated monetary policy to political pressure and lost the inflation anchor. Her February 'Made in Europe' speech is more structural and economic, focused on European scale and competitiveness via a '28th regime' for the Single Market, without advocating for easier monetary conditions. Two of her four speeches were PDF-only (Mar 27 'Monetary policy in times of geopolitical fragmentation' and Feb 18 'Fiscal challenges amid geopolitical uncertainty') — both titles suggest continuation of her hawkish-vigilant framing. Score held stable: insufficient new readable content to move it, but no evidence of any dovish shift.
representative_quote: In a world marked by more frequent supply-side shocks, the main challenge of central banks, regardless of their mandate, is to preserve a credible commitment to price stability.
